entry.title: Re: Native Promotion Engine Crashes Due to Integer Overflow When numberOfUnits Exceeds Integer.MAX_V
entry.content: We confirm the same issue on our project. The problem is reproducible when order entry quantities exceed Integer.MAX_VALUE and a promotion is triggered. The cart calculation crashes with IllegalArgumentException: The numberOfUnits cannot be negative! From our side too, this points to a native Promotion Engine limitation / defect rather than a project-specific issue. Thanks for posting this. We hope SAP can confirm the defect and provide an official correction

entry.title: Re: Configuration Differences When SAP Datasphere Is Inte...
entry.content:
Hi, so you already "rewired" your DSP and it became a formation with the BDC (location ID...). 

Connection remain the same as well as all replication flows. From LT nor from Azure any changes are needed. 

Is there anything missing? Not working?

entry.title: Re: How to get assignmentEndDate field to Variable Pay Assignment Details section
entry.content: @swagatikapanda Bring over the salary data into an ALCF then take the ID of the ALCF field and paste it in the formula row of a custom column at summary level. Doing this always returns the latest information of all assignments, including those that don't end on 12/31

entry.title: Re: AI Developer Challenge - Week 1: Grounding with SAP Generative AI Hub
entry.content:
Hi, good day! @noravonthenen here the Output cell from week 1... Thanks! Excellent start!

"Die Tafel" (literally "The Table") is the common name for a network of **food banks** in Germany. It is a crucial social initiative that plays a significant role in combating food waste and supporting people in need.

Here's a breakdown of what "die Tafel" is:

1. **Purpose:**
* **Combating Food Waste:** They collect surplus, perfectly edible food that would otherwise be thrown away by
supermarkets, bakeries, manufacturers, and other donors.
* **Providing Food Assistance:** They distribute this collected food to individuals and families who are experiencing financial hardship.

2. **How it Works:**
* **Collection:** Volunteers pick up food items that are still good but can no longer be sold (e
.g., due to approaching best-before dates, damaged packaging, or overproduction).
* **Distribution:** The food is then sorted and distributed at various Tafel locations (often called "Tafel-Ausgabestellen" or "Tafel-Läden") to registered clients.
*
**Symbolic Fee:** Clients usually pay a very small, symbolic fee (e.g., 1 or 2 Euros) for a bag of groceries, which helps cover some operational costs.

3. **Who it Helps:**
* Individuals and families who are experiencing poverty or financial hardship. This
includes:
* Recipients of social benefits (e.g., Bürgergeld, Wohngeld).
* Low-income earners.
* Pensioners with small pensions.
* Single parents.
* Homeless individuals.
* Refugees and asylum
seekers.
* To receive food from die Tafel, individuals usually need to provide proof of their financial need (e.g., benefit statements, income statements).

4. **Key Characteristics:**
* **Volunteer-Driven:** The vast majority of Tafel operations are run by dedicated volunteers who donate
their time to collect, sort, and distribute food.
* **Non-Profit:** Die Tafel organizations operate on a non-profit basis and rely heavily on donations (food, financial, and in-kind).
* **Nationwide Network:** There are over 960 Tafel locations across Germany,
making it a widespread and accessible support system.

5. **Significance:**
* Die Tafel provides essential food support, helping to alleviate hunger and improve the nutritional situation of many vulnerable people.
* It also raises awareness about food waste and promotes sustainable consumption.

If you or someone you
know needs assistance from die Tafel, it's best to visit their official website or contact your local Tafel directly to inquire about their specific eligibility requirements, registration process, and opening hours.

**Official Website:** [www.tafel.de](https://www.tafel.de)

entry.title: Re: SAP Business Data Cloud and Datasphere News in January
entry.content:
Hello @kpsauer 

We tested the new option in the Analytical Model to create a restriction measure using the new YTD() function, but when I check the data, it always appears empty (“-”).

The code I am using is "PostingDate" = YTD('YQMD'), and my PostingDate is a Time Dimension.

Could you please let me know if I might be doing something wrong, or if there are any specific considerations regarding the Time Dimension that I should take into account? Additionally, could you share any further documentation on how to use this function?

Thank you in advance for your help.

André Lopes

entry.title: Re: Data persistence in Datasphere
entry.content:
Persistency will not open ASSOC. Assoc by definition are temporary and will be used by AM only. Not view persisting. 

It has to show 750k. Try to copy the view into a very new one. I have had the issue of obsolete version in runtime (I was deploying to PROD however) and I was reading such issues were not uncommon in DWC. What helped me was to copy the view... 

Try also without any associations if not sure. 

The inflation of 1:n View-Assoc happens in AM

entry.title: Re: Excluding specific PO lines from a header condition amount
entry.content: Do you mean certain condition types from PO header when you say " specific PO line items" from header condition amount?

entry.title: Re: Identifying and mitigating potential DDoS attacks in SAP Commerce Cloud
entry.content: I usually start by watching for sudden traffic spikes in the access logs, then set tighter rate limits and block suspicious IP ranges. Enabling detailed monitoring helps spot patterns quicker

entry.title: Re: Document Migration: Understanding User Difference Between Onboarding 1.0 and BizX
entry.content:
Dear @GabiMoraru and @Sue-Lhymn ,

We have improved the migration process with a report-based approach. Kindly check this KBA 3737975 - Documents Assigned to Admin User After Migration from Onboarding 1.0 - Onboarding - SAP for Me

@GabiMoraru For analysis on what could be the configuration issue, only a root cause via ticket can help. But the standard configuration should generate no issues, once the sync should happen naturally (Via OnboardingCandidateInfo (OCI))

Best regards,
Jacson
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Week 2 – Building AI Agents with CrewAI and Generative AI Hub

Provided good extensive response using Claude 4.5 sonnet from Gen AI Hub (some examples below).

entry.title: Blank PDF issue in SAP Fiori apps: Fix
entry.content:
Hi All

During my work with SAP UI5 applications, I newly faced an issue where PDF files won't open and instead displayed an empty screen in the browser which avoids users from accessing/ printing required documents. Upon analyzing, I identified that certain browser settings in Edge and Chrome were causing the issue.

Fix: 

To fix the blank PDF screen issue, I modified below settings in Edge and Chrome. These changes helped PDFs are managed to open within the browser environment.

Edge:

Chrome:

In the Site Settings, check if Pop-ups are allowed (if not, change the setting)

(old site settings screenshot)

After updating these changes, the PDF documents opened rightly without an empty screen. These settings assist the browser process and display PDF content from SAP UI5 apps.

The blank screen issue when opening PDFs in SAP UI5 applications can relate to browser configuration than the application itself. By adjusting the necessary settings in Microsoft Edge and Google Chrome, the issue can be fixed. Also, these changes are not limited to SAP UI5 and can be applied to other web applications facing similar PDF display issues.

Note: This blog post is originally written based on my personal experience and knowledge. I have ensured that the content reflects my own understanding.

Thank you!!
BR// Dhanasupriya Sidagam

entry.title: Re: Output Parameters for Handling Unit Label Printing in...
entry.content: I just checked in our S/4HANA public cloud system and the enhancement spot SDR_EXTOM_OUTPUT_DATA_ENHANCE does not exist (in developer extensibility).

entry.title: Re: workflow for manage recurring journal entries
entry.content:
Dear @MP_77,

There is no standard workflow/approval process for Manage Recurring Journal Entries. SAP states this explicitly workflow is available only for Verify General Journal Entries, not for recurring or reversing journal entries

More details below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3430254

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3607875

The standard recurring-entry process is:

  1. Create the template in Manage Recurring Journal Entries
  2. Go to Schedule General Ledger Jobs
  3. Schedule the job Post Recurring Journal Entries
  4. Maintain the required selection parameters, including Due Date. SAP explains that recurring entries after the Due Date are not posted and the recommended setting is Due Date must cover the last planned posting date

More details below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3035119

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3022472

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0fa84c9d9c634132b7c4abb9ffdd8f06/cd189f57e7356d10e10000000a441470.html

If the business requirement is approval before posting the standard SAP route is Verify General Journal Entries because SAP also confirms that workflow verification must be done through the Verify General Journal Entries suite, not through Post General Journal Entries and not through recurring entries

Summary:

For recurring journal entries in Public Cloud the process is create recurring entry, schedule Post Recurring Journal Entries job and monitor job result. A separate approval workflow for the recurring-entry object itself is not supported in standard

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA - Empowering Your Homepage: Enabling My Home for SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS0
entry.content: Will there be as well an option for SAP S/4HANA Foundation products to use the new my Home? In SAP GTS Edition for SAP HANA it is not available.

entry.title: SAP Fiori/CAP – Draft enabled on entity but Edit/Delete not visible in Fiori elements object page
entry.content:
Hi all,

I’m currently working with CAP OData V4 service used in a Fiori elements application.

I’m trying to enable editing (Edit button + Create/Delete for child entities) on the object page of an entity called Messages, but I’m running into issues with draft handling.

Current situation

I have enabled draft on the entity:

@odata.draft.enabled
entity Messages {
  key ID : UUID;
  // fields...
}

However, in the generated Fiori elements app for the Customer entity (from which I navigate to the Messages, as seen below in the Data model):

  • The Edit button is not visible on the Messages object page
  • Create/Delete is missing for its child composition

Data model (simplified)

entity Customer {
  supplyChains : Composition to many SupplyChains;
}

entity SupplyChains {
  messages : Association to many Messages;
}

@odata.draft.enabled
entity Messages {
  headers : Composition to many OtherEntity;
}

Assumption / Question

From my understanding, Fiori elements (OData V4) relies heavily on draft + composition hierarchies for enabling full CRUD functionality.

So I suspect the issue is caused by this part:

Association to many Messages

instead of a composition.

My questions

  1. Is a fully composition-based hierarchy required (Customer → SupplyChains → Messages) for draft editing to work properly in Fiori elements?
  2. If I keep the association between SupplyChains and Messages:
    • Is it still possible to enable Edit on the Messages object page?
    • Are there recommended patterns for this scenario?
  3. Do I need to enable @odata.draft.enabled on all parent entities, or only on Messages and its children?

Additional info

  • Service is exposed as OData V4
  • Fiori elements app generated in BAS
  • Messages is exposed via projection in the service

Any clarification on how draft + composition vs association affects UI editability in CAP would be highly appreciated.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition error message for i...
entry.content:
Dear @kanchane_jayasinghe36,

In Public Cloud SSCUI 102320 / Define Output Format of Material Numbers is the correct configuration activity, but changing the product/material number length from 18 to 40 characters is only possible after SAP activates Product Number Length Extension in the tenant. SAP Public Cloud documentation states that this activation is a prerequisite and only afterward can the new length be maintained in SSCUI 102320

More details below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3560881

https://help.sap.com/doc/ce01d82756b947a1a043a5d5a3204226/2502.06/en-US/WN_CE2502.1_EN.pdf

SAP Note 2696446 is consistent with that prerequisite. It explains what happens once the length setting is used including that the Material Number and Old Material Number are set with the same length and that the Old Material Number retains the highest length ever configured but it does not remove the need for SAP activation first. SAP also states that, once the length has been extended, it must not be reduced again

So based on the attached error the tenant is still validating against the currently effective shorter length so this should first be treated as an activation / tenant-status issue, not as a missing CBC configuration option. The recommended next step is for SAP to verify whether Product Number Length Extension is already active in the affected tenant. If it is not active, SAP needs to activate it first. If it is already active and SSCUI 102320 still raises BMG223 this should be handled by SAP as a tenant-specific support issue

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: Where is the "follow user" option in the new Community?
entry.content:
Hi Everyone,

I have been looking for a way to follow people on SAP (sites) and was able to do it via below workaround.

Step1: Identify the user ID of the person you want to follow - either via a Q&A post, comment or blog post.
Step2: Construct this URL - https://profile.sap.com/u/{userid}
For Example:  https://profile.sap.com/u/VXLozano
Step3: Click "Follow" as per the screenshot below

Hope this helps!

entry.title: Joule Studio: Why is build_default required if standard plan exists?
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

I’m currently setting up Joule Studio in SAP BTP and reviewing the prerequisites.

According to the documentation, Joule Studio requires an entitlement for SAP Build Process Automation with the service plan “build_default”.

However, in my subaccount I already have the “standard” (standard-application) plan assigned, which provides full production capabilities for SAP Build Process Automation (workflows, RPA, monitoring, etc.).

From my understanding:

  • The standard plan offers full functionality for productive use

  • The build_default plan seems to provide a more limited or base-level feature set

This raises a concern, as introducing or switching to build_default could potentially limit existing capabilities.

My questions are:

  1. Is the build_default plan technically required for Joule Studio, even if the standard plan is already available?

  2. Is there a dependency or integration requirement that specifically relies on build_default?

  3. Can both plans coexist without impacting existing Build Process Automation scenarios?

  4. What is the recommended entitlement setup for using Joule Studio in a productive BTP environment?

Any clarification or best practices would be highly appreciated.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: How to set up combine Multiple Sales Schedule Agreement as one Delivery Order
entry.content:
1. I create 2 Sales Schedule Agreements with the same Customer, Material and Schedule Line Date

2. When I use app : Create Outbound Delivery with Order Reference 

I can combine multiple Sales Schedule Agreements into one Delivery Order 

3. But when I use app : Sales Order Schedule Lines

I can't combine multiple Sales Schedule Agreements into one Delivery Order and Customer is already flagged for the "Order Combination" in the Customer Master Data

Could you please help or suggest how to set up a combined Multiple Sales Schedule Agreement as one Delivery Order?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to set up combine Multiple Sales Schedule Agreeme...
entry.content:
Dear customer,

Hope you are doing great.

We have identified that the behavior is related to how the app “Sales Order Schedule Lines – Due for Delivery” processes delivery creation depending on the execution mode.

In this app, there are two different processing options:

1. Dialog (Create single delivery in dialog)

  • The system processes each selected document individually
  • As a result, only one outbound delivery per reference document is created
  • Combination of multiple Sales Scheduling Agreements is not supported in this mode

2. Background

  • The system processes documents via backend logic (function module RV_DELIVERY_CREATE)
  • In this case, multiple documents can be passed together
  • Therefore, order combination is supported, and a single outbound delivery can be created

This is standard system behavior:

  • Dialog -> no combination (one delivery per document)
  • Background -> combination possible

If your business scenario requires combining multiple Sales Scheduling Agreements into one delivery, we recommend using:

  • The Background option in Sales Order Schedule Lines – Due for Delivery, or
  • The app “Create Outbound Delivery with Order Reference”, which supports combination by design

Hope the above clarifies your queries.

Best regards,
Letícia

entry.title: Hide fields in PDC step in onboarding using business rules
entry.content:
Hello, I am trying to make certain personal info fields not visible to US onboardees, but keep them visible to NDL onboardees. Here is an example of the rule and fields I am trying to hide but it is not working as expected, and the field is still visible for the US.

Are we able to use business rules to hide certain fields in the PDC step?

entry.title: Re: How to Connect Eclipse with Git or abapGit and Push A...

entry.title: Re: Document Migration: Understanding User Difference Between Onboarding 1.0 and BizX
entry.content:
Dear @Sue-Lhymn 

The bulk download sends a zip file with no identification on file name itself.

Each zip file will have the documents and a index file. So you will have to check the CSV index file to identify the files.

You need to start / test a bulk download to see information as there is no pre-report.

Best regards,
Jacson
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: XML for Adobe Master template
entry.content:
A work around will be to put a break point within the CL_SOMU_FORM_SERVICES class.

That has to be done through the ADT tool. "Eclipse"

Within the method "get_document" put a breakpoint after  "__get_data( ".

Execute the output, when the debugger will open, select the variable ev_xml. It will contain the XML including the master form template section.

Make sure to remove the limitation of the variable display when the breakpoint stop.

Right click on the variable, select Show Detail as -> Default View

Right click in the view box, select "Max Length" and set it to "0" => illimited

Right click again on the variable "ev_xml", select "Show Detail as" -> XML view

Now you have the full XML including the Form master data.

entry.title: Re: EWM – STO using same HU number
entry.content:
Hi @krisli1 ,

Even in Warehouse 1000, you can still receive the HU using an internal number range, but the HU number must stay within the defined range threshold.

Best Regards,

Yuri Taranto
SAP EWM Consultant

entry.title: Re: Synchronizing Purchase Scheduling Agreements with TM Transport Planning
entry.content:
Great blog. Why do you say synchronisation report would be an issue when there are partially delivered schedule lines?

Ideal flow should be, i create schedule lines from MRP, Run optimizer, update my schedule line and then communicate my supplier with updates schedule line and he sends an ASN in return when shopping. 

Can't I have controls to secure the dates of freight units or schedule lines that are fixed, or linked to a delivery? Thereby open schedule lines are still open to be edited to counter sudden increase or decrease in schedule line.

You seem to have invested a lot of time in this? Would like to discuss more on this if you're willing.

Email - ranjithncbe@gmail.com

entry.title: Re: ServiceNow being an external catalog for SAP Ariba Bu...
entry.content:
Ariba’s external catalog requirements are strict. A catalog must support:

  • OCI punchout (Open Catalog Interface)

  • Return of line-level data in OCI format

  • Supplier part number, description, price, UOM, UNSPSC

  • cXML wrapper compatibility

  • HTTPS endpoint with session handling

  • Ability to return a “cart” back to Ariba Guided Buying

  • No workflow or approval logic embedded in the external catalog

  • No dynamic pricing unless supported via OCI hooks

ServiceNow’s native catalog is designed for ITSM, not procurement. It does not ship with OCI punchout or cXML return capabilities by default.

ServiceNow Store App (Punchout Integration) — if licensed: Some customers use a ServiceNow Punchout plugin or partner-built connector that adds:

  • OCI outbound

  • OCI return

  • cXML envelope

  • Cart mapping

  • Session token handling

This makes ServiceNow behave like a compliant external catalog.

Note: ServiceNow is not an SAP-certified external catalog out of the box. However, it can function as one if the customer implements an OCI-compliant punchout integration that meets SAP Ariba’s external catalog requirements. Without this, Ariba Guided Buying remains the recommended and supported front-end for initiating IT service or item requests.

entry.title: Re: OData v2 upsert on custom MDF Object creates record but does NOT trigger workflow

entry.title: FAGLB03 Default Variant is not defaulting
entry.content:
User is running into an issue where their default layout for FAGLB03 is changing based on the last variant chosen when running FAGLL03.
User reports using a specific variant each day for a revenue process that is completed in T-code FAGLL03.
When user runs T-code FAGLB03, the last variant layout from t-code FAGLL03 comes over by default. We checked their default layout in FAGLB03, and it says that it is set to 1SAP, but it is not defaulting to that layout.
Please see attached testing document for examples of this occurring.

entry.title: Re: Recurring Deduction Integration in SAP EC: Challenges, Limitations & Workaround
entry.content: Thanks Xaheer, this is very useful. I suggest you create an SAP Influence request highlighting the need to enable Recurring Deductions objects to be API-enabled. You can specify the business justification, e.g. employers receiving information from Salary Packaging or Car leasing providers. And also, enabling the API would eliminate the need to round trip data to and from CPI.

entry.title: Re: Error while trying to deploying Fiori list report to ABAP on SAP Public Cloud

entry.title: 3012667 - Without the ILM Business Function being active in the system, BUPA_ILM_BF
entry.content:
Hi Team, 

Good day!

Need your help in implementing the below note: 

3012667 - Business Function (BF) BUPA_ILM_BF cannot be activated without BF ILM being active in the system.

In our system, the BUPA_ILM_BF business function is activated; however, the ILM business function is not active. According to SAP Note 3012667, the system states that “Without the ILM Business Function being active in the system, BUPA_ILM_BF should not be activated.”

This note was identified in the Simplification Item List as part of the upgrade.

What would be the impact if ILM Business Function is not activated in the system.

Could you please help clarify whether the required activity needs to be performed in the upgraded system or in the existing (pre-upgrade) system?

If this needs to be performed in the pre-upgraded system, i would need your help in guiding us in the activation of it.

Regards,

entry.title: Is class_constructor a good method to place the buffer in?
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

 

As the subject suggests. I'm trying to understand what the best method is for placing the buffer.

I know that Class_constructor can't be used with a WHERE clause; therefore, the buffer must read the whole table. I'm not sure if this is suitable for some cases or not, based on the size of the table. I would love a clear explanation of this topic.

SAP S/4HANA Cloud ABAP Environment

Chris

entry.title: Triggering SAP CPI iflow through SAP Build Process Automation
entry.content:
This blog is for highlighting the basics of using SAP Build Process Automation and how it can be leveraged in various business scenarios. For demo purpose, I have created SAP CPI iflow which gets triggered by using a Automation job created in SAP BPA.

 

  1. Create CPI iflow test_iflow as below to capture data from Northwind opensource odata services with HTTPs sender adapter- 

 2. Activate the CPI iflow - 

 3. Create Destination in SAP BTP account using oauth credentials (client-id, client-secret created in BTP account) 

 

4. Add the created Destination in SAP Build->Control_Tower as below-  

 

 5. Create ‘Project’ ztestingprj_iflow under Connectors->Actions as below-  

 

 

 6.

a.  Create ‘Action’ zjoule_action under project ‘ztestingprj_iflow’ with URL [/http/ztest_joule] from endpoint generated from CPI iflow ‘test_iflow’. 

b. In the ‘Output’ tab, we will capture only some part of the response payload in the Body as the result.

c. In Destination, select the destination which was created in BTP 

d. Click Test. The Action triggers CPI iflow ‘test_iflow’ generating response [200 OK] with payload generated by CPI iflow.  

 

 7. Create ‘Process Automation’ project zProcess_prj under ‘Lobby’  

 

8. In Process Automation project created above; create Automation Artifact zauto1_sampleprocess as below- 

 

 9. In Automation Artifact created above, drag and drop the Action created in Step 6. 

 

 10. In ‘Project Properties’ create Environment Variable of type ‘destination’ as below and Save the  Automation Artifact- 

 11. To run the Automation in Desktop Agent create Automation Launcher zAutoLaunch1 as below- 

 

 12. In Automation Launcher created above drag and drop the Automation Artifact created in step 8. 

 13. Save the project. Release the required version and Deploy in ‘Public’ environment as below- 

 

 14. During Deployment, select the Destination created in BTP (step 3) in the Environment variable created in step 10.  

Click Deploy. 

 

 15. After successful Deployment the deployed version could be seen in the path as below- 

 16. Download Desktop Agent from SAP Development Tools as below and install in your system- 

 

 17. In Desktop Agent, click Add Tenant and register your SAP BPA account as below- 

 18. For Automation, set the Desktop Agent in ‘Unattended Mode’ as below- 

 

 19. After successful Tenant registration in Desktop Agent; we can see its entry in Agent Management under Control Tower path as below- 

 20. In ‘Triggers’ tab, create a trigger as per required schedule- 

 

 21. We can observe automatic triggering of the CPI iflow with the response payload- 

 22. In Monitoring->Automation Jobs; we can see the entries as per scheduled timings of trigger- 

 

 23. On Activating Traces, we can see the Traces showing response payloads coming from CPI iflow-

entry.title: Slicenceの仮（Temp）キーについて
entry.content:
DEVシステムログイン時、ライセンス有効期限にかかわるワーニングが表示されるため
Slicenceにて確認したところ、仮ライセンスの期限がもうすぐ終了するものの、永久ライセンスがすでに追加済でした。永久ライセンスのステータスはInactiveです。
この場合、仮ライセンスの削除が正しい操作でしょうか？
スクリーンショットにて、Slicenceの状況を共有させていただきます


--- Steps to Reproduce ---

１．Slicenceにてライセンスの情報を取得



------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Slicenceの仮（Temp）キーについて
entry.content:
本事象について、以下の手順によって実施してください。

1. System 850853030にてHardware Key W0440932934で新しいキーを作成してください。

2. Slicenseですべての古いキーを削除してください。

3. Slicenseで新しいキーをインストールしてください。

そうすると、問題を解決するはずです。もしそれ以上の質問がある場合、遠慮なくお問い合わせください。

entry.title: What are the required authorizations to use the SSCR Keys application?
entry.content:
What are the required authorizations to use the SSCR Keys application?

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: What are the required authorizations to use the SSCR...
entry.content:
To use the SSCR Keys application, your S-user ID must have the appropriate authorizations:
  • Register Object and Developer Keys
  • Register Object Keys

To request authorizations, use the Self-service Authorization Request - KBA 2891528

 To speed up processing for S-user ID authorization requests, I recommend contacting the existing administrators listed under Important Contacts within your company to approve your request.

For more detailed information, please refer to the KBAs below:

2453897 - How to request Object and/or Developer Keys (SSCR) for On-premise - SAP for Me

2489468 - Installation number is missing from the SSCR application - SAP for Me

2891528 - How to use the Self-Service Authorization Request - SAP for Me

How to speed up processing for S-user ID authorization requests
Contact your company's Administrators who are listed in Important Contacts.

If you have any questions, please feel free to contact us.

Best regards,
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Week 3 - Use AI Agents with Grounding Tools
entry.content: Output

entry.title: Problem reinstalling SAP ASE
entry.content:
Hi, ALL,

A long time ago I downloaded and successfully installed SAP ASE 16.0 SP3 on one of my Linux boxes.

 

Now since that install becomes unusable, I removed the directory and trying to install it again from scratch.

 

I rerun the setup.bin and went through the wizard and then I went thru the configuration wizard. And then it just stays there.

 

I tried to run the installer in the console mode and it turns out that it is stuck in the server build procedure.

 

What could be the issue? I can post a log file from the install - just tell me which one.

 

Thank you for the help and any pointer.

entry.title: How to Archive in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition?
entry.content:
HI, Dears
According to our system's license analysis, the following actions are recommended in priority order.
Could you please provide guidance on how to implement these actions or archive data in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition?

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 
#Archive Data
SAP Information Lifecycle Management 

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Does PRIMARY KEY clustered check stored somewhere
entry.content:
Given the more narrow scope of the query  results, you should be able to get that from just SYSTAB and SYSIDX - avoiding SYS.SYSINDEXES .   This would remove the CHAR column join on index name as well as the fact that there is an ASE compatibility view DBO.SYSINDEXES that has a different result set.  This should solve the query problem in a simple 2 table query (my previous suggestion is more general reporting for multiple tables and reporting the columns of the indexes that are clustered).  The query, shown below, returns clustered primary key, if defined, for a specific table. Removing the last predicate will return a list of tables that have a clustered primary key.

select
      st.table_name as TabName
    , st.clustered_index_id as ClusteredIdxID
    , si.index_name as IdxName
    , 'Primary Key' as IdxType  
from 
    sys.systab st join sys.sysidx si
where
        st.clustered_index_id = si.index_id -- clustered index
    and si.index_category = 1 -- primary key
    and st.table_name = 't_clustered_1'

entry.title: Re: Unlocking the Future: Accelerated transformation to SAP Cloud ERP Private with SAP’s new service
entry.content: As I understand it, the recent PCE contract covers the Transform preparation service. Could you let me know the point of contact for delivering this to the customer? thanks.

entry.title: CDS View Annotations for List Object Page,
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I have a List-Object page where  I need to display Harvey Pie Chart . The requirement is to show Spent Amt  from Total Amt. 

When  Total Amt is put in the maximum valve , the CDS view give an error :Annotation value 'TotalAmt' must be of type 'DecimalFloat': I was not able to resolve the issue even if it was coming from standard SAP amount field and referencing to Currency. 

To troubleshoot and simplify , I took two fields in CDS i.e. ActualValue and MaxValue  and default as abap.dec( 15, 2 ) to 25.00  and 100.00 respectively. The CDS view starting complaining about the maximum valve: 'MaxValue'   is not DecimalFloat at @ui.dataPoint ; then I gave maximum valve: 100.00 as shown in screenshot below. 

But the UI is showing as "No data" and console is giving error as  "The DataPoint annotation is empty. Please check it! ".

I tried using google/SDN/AI tool to analyze the issue but no luck so far.  Can someone please direct me to right direction. 

PS : I was able to get the progress bar with the same field but was not to get the Harvey Pie Chart. which is my requirement. 

  • CDCS View with @ui.chart

  • CDCS View with @ui.dataPoint 

  • Object List Page showing "No Data "  :

  • Console Error : 

SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA

entry.title: unable to locate the path where billing plan type 03 is mainained from the attched screen
entry.content:
unable  to locate the path where billing plan type 03 is maintained from  the attached screen .

Kindly share the path 

i was unable to find in below 2 paths

IMG > Sales and Distribution > Billing > Payment Cards > Maintain Payment Card Plan Types.

IMG > Sales and Distribution > Billing > Billing plan  > Define billing plan .

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud enables user-based authoriza...
entry.content:
Hi Kerisha,

I also create a ticket to SAP in parallel.

The expert provides the following information. It looks the Sales Order Net Value field is not part of the price elements area controlled by access levels. I'll further align with developer to check if there is solution to cover the requirements/

You can find the detailed feedback as below.

Regarding your business requirement, after debugging in SAP Backend, I created the documentation below to explain to you the behavior of our coding logic regarding the view restriction of fields:

  • 0003744032 - Authorization restrictions in the field Net Value - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

As per SAP standard documentation, role-based access to price elements applies only to pricing elements in the price elements area such as condition types and assigned subtotals (for example discounts, internal cost, margin).

Sales Order Net Value field (header and item) is a document total and is not part of the price elements area controlled by access levels. Therefore, Net Value cannot be hidden via access level configuration. This behavior applies consistently in Manage Sales Orders – Version 2 as well as in the Create Sales Orders - VA01 app.

If an user is authorized to display or approve a sales order, the Net Value will be visible by design. There is no standard configuration, authorization object, or authorization check in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition to suppress Net Value visibility for sales orders. 

Since development tracks the customers ideas via the SAP Customer Influence portal I kindly ask you to create a feature request for this business requirement.

Best regards,

Joey

entry.title: Re: 1H 2026 Release: Streamlining Data after Employment Terminations in SuccessFactors Employee Cent

entry.title: Configuring custom GRC NWBC Launch Screens restricted via PFCG roles
entry.content:
Challenge: Standard SAP GRC roles for NetWeaver Business Client (NWBC) grant excessive access privileges that exceed users' actual job requirements. This over-provisioning creates security risks and poor user experience, as users encounter numerous irrelevant hyperlinks and folders they don't need for their daily tasks.

Implementation Approach: The solution leverages transaction LPD_CUST (Configure Launchpad for Menus) to design custom NWBC landing pages. This involves copying standard Webdynpro application configuration, component configuration and UIBB component in which LPD_CUST role is linked.

In this blog, the detailed steps for configuring LPD_CUST, Web Dynpro Configuration and PFCG mapping is highlighted.

Environment: GRC Access Control 12.0

Launchpad Flow chart:

 

Section 1: LPD_CUST launchpad customisation

In Tcode LPD_CUST, we need to define the customization that is required for the workset, here we have considered GRACHOME (GRC Access Control Home Workset). It is recommended to take a copy of this workset to custom naming convention to preserve standard configuration.

Select line item GRACHOME, Click on copy.

 

 

Enter your custom values and namespace.

 

 

In ZGRACHOME, You can make all the required customisations. Here, for simplicity, I have modified the folder name to ‘Work Inbox - CUSTOM’.

Potential Use case 1: For Approvers users, you can keep only ‘Work Inbox’, Delete ‘Work Inbox -  Simplified’ to make it more clear to users as to which one to use.

Potential Use case 2: In Application help, You can place company’s intranet URL link that points to a guide on how to review and approve requests. For this, you can create ‘New Application’ , select application type ‘URL’ and add the required URL.

 

You can follow the same steps to customize Setup, Access Management, Reports and Analytics Webdynpro Application

 

Section 2: NWBC Webdynpro customisation

In tcode PFCG, copy standard role SAP_GRAC_NWBC to custom role Z_TEST_NWBC,

Tcode PFCG -> Z_TEST_NWBC

Here, we are customizing the ‘My Home’ Webdynpro application GRFN_SERVICE_MAP.

Right click on ‘My Home’ Webdynpro application -> Details

We see the standard Configuration: GRAC_FPM_AC_LPD_HOME. We want to customize this Application Configuration to point our desired ‘My Home’ Layout in NWBC which we configured in LPD_CUST.

Click on ‘Application Configuration’ button.

 

This will launch a web URL which takes you to application configuration.

 

 

From here on, the logic is that we copy the Application Configuration, Component Configuration, UIBB Configuration and In UIBB configuration we point it to Role /instance of LPD_CUST. Refer to the flowchart.

Step 1: Create Copy of Application Configuration.

 
Click on Other Functions -> New Window -> Copy

 

 

 

Provide custom configuration ID and description

 

Select the relevant package, here I chose $TMP (Non-transportable), Click OK.

 

 

Click Change

 

We enter into the custom application configuration Z1GRAC_FPM_AC_LPD_HOME

Click on hyper link GRAC_FPM_CC_LPD_HOME

 

You will reach Component Configuration screen as below

 

Step 2: Create copy of component configuration

Click on Other Functions -> New Window -> Copy

 

Provide the custom configuration ID and description

 

Enter the desired package

 

Click Change

 

Click on Edit -> Cancel to get in Display mode, Click on hyperlink ‘GRAC_FPM_UIBB_LPD_HOME’

 

Step 3: Create copy of UIBB configuration

In this window, Click on Other Functions -> New Window

Click Copy

 

Provide the custom configuration ID and description

 

Click Change.

In this window, we need to map ZGRACHOME in Role field (earlier created in LPD_CUST).

Change it to ZGRACHOME. All the customizations done in ZGRACHOME can be previewed. Notice that we had changed folder ‘Work Inbox – CUSTOM’ in this scenario.

Click Save.

Select the TR and Click OK

 

Section 3: PFCG Role Configuration mapping

Step 1: In tcode PFCG, Role Z_TEST_NWBC

Right click on ‘My Home’ -> Details -> Change configuration to Z1GRAC_FPM_AC_LPD_HOME

Click Save

 

Step 2: Again, Right click on ‘My Home’ -> Details -> Click Application Configuration.

 

This will open up URL in web browser, we see that we are in custom application configuration Z1GRAC_FPM_AC_LPD_HOME, we see standard component configuration is mapped, we will need to change to custom component configuration Z1GRAC_FPM_CC_LPD_HOME

 

Click on Edit -> Assign Configuration Name

Assign the custom component configuration ID Z1GRAC_FPM_CC_LPD_HOME

Click Save.

 

Step 3: Click the hyperlink Z1GRAC_FPM_CC_LPD_HOME

 

This will open up Custom component configuration, we see that standard UIBB is mapped, but we need to change to custom UIBB Z1GRAC_FPM_UIBB_LPD_HOME

 

Click on F4 button on GRAC_FPM_UIBB_LPD_HOME, change it to Z1GRAC_FPM_UIBB_LPD_HOME

Click Save

 

Step 4: Click on Edit -> Cancel. We are in display mode. Click on hyperlink Z1GRAC_FPM_UIBB_LPD_HOME

 

You will find all the elements mapped as per the flowchart

 

Assign the PFCG role Z_TEST_NWBC to user and test the changes. Or you can Right click on ‘My Home’ -> Execute.

 

The customisation done in LPD_CUST reflects. 

NOTE: If you assign standard role SAP_GRAC_NWBC and custom role Z_TEST_NWBC together to a user, this won’t work as intended since the system is confused whether to pick up the standard application configuration or the custom application configuration defined in the Webdynpro application GRFN_SERVICE_MAP. The intent of creating the custom role is to assign that specific custom role Z_TEST_NWBC to satisfy all NWBC access requirements for the user.

You can follow the same steps to customize Setup, Access Management, Reports and Analytics Webdynpro Application.

 

Conclusion:

You have now successfully created a custom PFCG role with Web Dynpro application configuration pointing to Custom NWBC screen.

entry.title: Re: Compliance form did not trigger for external candidat...
entry.content: Can you check in ODATA API Data Dictionary if the entities "PerAddressDEFLT" and "FOCorporateAddressDEFLT" have the Field "stateNav" ? If it does not exist, then you will have to ensure that this is added. If it exists and the compliance forms are still not triggering, I would advise you to create a SAP ticket.

entry.title: Re: Settlement through report - RKO7KO8G
entry.content: Basically the settlement depends upon the Variant created for settlement run. The points indicated by Lakshmipathi are maintained in the variant. You may choose these indicators, as applicable to FI conditions.

entry.title: Re: Hide fields in PDC step in onboarding using business...
entry.content:
Hello, 
I see that the rule scenario you have used is "onView rules for HRIS", this is not supported in onboarding pages. You can configure an onInIt rule using "Trigger onInit Rules for Hire/Rehire" or "Rules for Hire/Rehire Processes".

Regards,

Akarsh

entry.title: Re: Week 3 - Use AI Agents with Grounding Tools
entry.content:
Week 3 - Agent Creation and grounded response

Your User ID: GE306012 ( prev: AC300864U49)

entry.title: Re: AM_ASSET - Archiving for a specific asset number
entry.content: No, it is not possible to enable the asset number field in Production Mode for AM_ASSET archiving. SAP intentionally restricts that field to test runs only. In productive runs, the system enforces residence time and retention rules, so you cannot bypass them by selecting specific asset numbers.

entry.title: Total Divesture of Subsidiary relating COI reversal
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I am facing issue related to reversal of Investment and related adjustments of which posting level 30 in total divesture of Subsidiary scenario. I did configuration of first consolidation and subsequent consolidation in which goodwill is calculated in first period and doing total divesture in fourth period. I haven't referred standard process for consolidation process. I request you to help me for configuration of Total Divesture of subsidiary. In this scenario Parent is Paris and Subsidiary is Greece.

entry.title: Re: data transition validation the tool to validate business data after conversion
entry.content: Hi Boris, it's a great valuable post and this tool looks very useful tor us. We are migrating from ECC to S/4HANA with bluefield approach, the migration tool is CBS ET. my questions is: 1. can we use this tool separately only for data validation of migrated data from legacy ECC to S/4HANA? 2. For table comparison, if the data structure are different between ECC and S/4HANA, e.g., BSEG -> ACDOCA, or different fields in same table name, does the comparison will consider the structure changed? 3. If there are master data number changed in target S/4 system, e.g., GL account number/ Profit Center number changed. is it possible to build up a mapping in DTV for validation and comparison purpose? Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Re: How to Add Custom Field in Purchase Requisition Item in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud (Step-by-Step)
entry.content:
Nice walkthrough.

Note that this is also the recommended way for on-premise and private cloud S/4HANA.
Though the available contexts might be less, it is something to check first for adding custom fields because it is 100% upgrade safe.
The available business contexts are lacking just because the on-premise and private cloud versions are running behind on the public cloud.

When the business context is there this is indeed a quick, powerful, and safe way to add extensions.

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: An Introduction to the Data Transition Validation Tool
entry.content: Hi SEdelstein, it's a great valuable post and this tool looks very useful tor us. We are migrating from ECC to S/4HANA with bluefield approach, the migration tool is CBS ET. my questions is: 1. can we use this tool separately only for data validation of migrated data from legacy ECC to S/4HANA? 2. For table comparison, if the data structure are different between ECC and S/4HANA, e.g., BSEG -> ACDOCA, or different fields in same table name, does the comparison will consider the structure changed? 3. If there are master data number changed in target S/4 system, e.g., GL account number/ Profit Center number changed. is it possible to build up a mapping in DTV for validation and comparison purpose? Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Re: Best practices to follow during DTV implementation
entry.content:
Hi @abhyarthana, it's a great valuable post and this tool looks very useful tor us. We are migrating from ECC to S/4HANA with bluefield approach, the migration tool is CBS ET.

Here are some questions about about validation by DTV tool, may I have your advices:

1. can we use this tool separately only for data validation of migrated data from legacy ECC to S/4HANA?

2. For table comparison, if the data structure are different between ECC and S/4HANA, e.g., BSEG -> ACDOCA, or different fields in same table name, does the comparison/validation still working, and will consider the structure changed between this 2 systems?

 3. If there are master data number changed in target S/4 system, e.g., GL account number/ Profit Center number changed. is it possible to build up a mapping in DTV for validation and comparison purpose?

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Re: Business data validation using DTV tool
entry.content:
Hi @abhyarthana, it's a great valuable post and this tool looks very useful tor us. We are migrating from ECC to S/4HANA with bluefield approach, the migration tool is CBS ET.

Here are some questions about about validation by DTV tool, may I have your advices:

1. can we use this tool separately only for data validation of migrated data from legacy ECC to S/4HANA?

2. For table comparison, if the data structure are different between ECC and S/4HANA, e.g., BSEG -> ACDOCA, or different fields in same table name, does the comparison/validation still working, and will consider the structure changed between this 2 systems?

 3. If there are master data number changed in target S/4 system, e.g., GL account number/ Profit Center number changed. is it possible to build up a mapping in DTV for validation and comparison purpose?

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Re: Workflow Trigger for Credit Memo (Document Type DG) i...
entry.content:
Good day @rohini_dutta21 

Thank you for your question.

Question 1: Could you please confirm whether this requirement is feasible within the standard Public Cloud framework?

Answer: No, your requirement is not feasible within the standard Public Cloud framework. There is no supported standard workflow for documents posted through Create Outgoing Invoices using the Credit Memo option with document DG document typ.  However, output configuration for FI customer invoices which is posted from Create Outgoing Invoices and Upload Customer Open Items is supported in Public Cloud. So, if you are looking to send emails, forms, or output after posting, that is the standard area you should check. DG is the accounting document type for Customer Credit Memo in the relevant accounting document type mappings.

See the following references for more details.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/918bca53037f408f91a2295d04ac16bc/dfd480bf588447e3afa2bca80b5d21ad.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0e602d466b99490187fcbb30d1dc897c/73fe476d42454f5a95b1ee0ae7498d5e.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/26b51d1773a04bcea7d2cc029bca45b4/63576ca65b74494e9170db9cc023eea1.html?locale=en-US

 

Question 2: If not, is there any planned roadmap item or enhancement that would support this functionality in future releases?

Answer: There is currently no publicized roadmap that specifically adds a standard workflow for Credit Memo/DG documents. I would not assume this functionality is planned.

Summary/recommendation:

  • If your business requirement is notification or document dispatch after your posting, then evaluate standard output management as the supported alternative.

  • If you require a true approval or post creation workflow, you can raise it through Customer Influence.

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: SAP Ariba Category Management 2605 Release Highlights
entry.content:
Hello SAP Ariba Source to Contract Community, and welcome to this latest blog post on the exciting updates coming to SAP Ariba Category Management. We're excited to announce the latest updates to SAP Ariba Category Management in Q2 2026. These enhancements are designed with one goal in mind: to give you more control, visibility, and flexibility in how you manage your source-to-contract processes.

Let's walk through what's new and how these features can help your organization work smarter.

1. Versioning of Strategy and Plan Documents for Updates to Custom Tools [ARBCATA-570]

When you update custom tools in your strategy and plan documents, you need to know exactly what changed and when. Our new versioning feature for strategy and plan documents does exactly that.

Now, every time a category manager updates a custom tool that's part of an approved document, a new version is automatically created and tracked. Category management administrators can even decide whether these changes should trigger a reapproval process - giving you the governance you need without unnecessary bottlenecks.

The result is complete visibility into document history and consistent version control across all your strategy and planning tools.

 

2. Enhancements to Risk Management in the Risk Assessment Tool [ARBCATA-573]

Managing risk doesn't happen in isolation. That's why we've enhanced the Risk Assessment tool with smarter collaboration features.

Risk owners can now create and assign tasks directly within the tool, making it easier to track mitigation activities and hold teams accountable. We've also introduced new permissions that let collaborators manage risks and tasks assigned to them—with email and in-app notifications triggered whenever ownership changes.

This means faster follow-ups, clearer accountability, and better visibility into your risk mitigation efforts.

A new permission, Can manage own risks, is now available when adding collaborators to strategy and plan documents. You can assign this permission to users who are responsible for handling risks.

 

A new section, Tasks, is now available in the risk page of the Risk Assessment tool. As a risk owner or a user with the Can manage own risks permission, you can create new tasks and assign them to other users.

 

3. Ability for Category Managers to Manage Category Profile Data [ARBCATA-583]

Category management shouldn't bottleneck at one person. With our new category profile management capability, any category manager on your team can now create, edit, and manage category profile data—not just the lead manager. Simply add team members and assign the right permissions, and they'll have the access they need to keep your category information current and accurate.

 

 

4. Make Category Profile Data Work Across Your Organization

Your category profile contains valuable information - regulations, policies, spend channels and it should not be siloed. We've added two powerful capabilities:

a. Inclusion of Category Profile Data in Strategy and Plan Documents [ARBCATA-589]

Category profile data now flows directly into your strategy and plan documents, eliminating manual work and reducing errors.

 

b. Apply Category Profile Information Across Subcategories [ARBCATA-610]

You can now associate and inherit profile information—like laws, regulations, and policies—from parent categories to subcategories automatically, saving time and ensuring consistency across your entire category hierarchy.

A new action button, Apply to Subcategories, is now available to apply the respective category profile information to subcategories.

 

5. Ability to Associate Multiple Guided Sourcing Projects with Initiatives and Initiative Tasks [ARBCATA-591]

If you're using SAP Ariba Sourcing with guided sourcing capability, you now have more flexibility in how you organize your work.

You can now associate multiple guided sourcing projects with initiatives and tasks, giving you a cleaner way to organize and track sourcing activities without creating separate initiatives for every project. This keeps your initiative management simpler and your sourcing activities better aligned.

 

6. Support for Integration with External Spend Data APIs [ARBCATA-642]

Many organizations maintain their own spend data systems, and copying that data into SAP Ariba Category Management is tedious and error-prone.

With external spend data API integration, you can now connect your own spend data source directly to SAP Ariba Category Management. Once configured, the system automatically retrieves and displays your spend information—at category, subcategory, supplier, and classification levels—without any manual intervention.

This means faster insights, less duplicate effort, and better spend visibility using data you already trust.

The UI Card Mapping dropdown label under the Integrations tab is now updated to Spend Card Mapping.

 

A new section, Spend Data, is now available in the Integrations >Spend Card Mapping page. The Spend Data section includes a spend data source selection option, with available choices of Procurement data warehouse and a Customer-defined spend data source.

 

A new section, Spend Data, is now available in the Integrations >Spend Card Mapping page. The Spend Data section includes a spend data source selection option, with available choices of Procurement data warehouse and a Customer-defined spend data source.

These updates reflect our commitment to building solutions that adapt to how you work - but not the other way around. So go ahead and review these items.

And, stay tuned for more enhancements and continue to leverage SAP Ariba Category Management to drive business success!

entry.title: 🇮🇳 Joule Studio: Create Joule Skills and Agents Without Coding (Hyderabad, India)
entry.content:
This is the all-new SAP CodeJam for Joule Studio, a new tool in SAP Build that lets you create extensions to Joule so users can use SAP's digital assistant in their custom workflows.

The CodeJam is intended to introduce you to the Joule Studio tool so you can create your own:

  • Skills, which let you create predefined execution flows, triggered by a user's prompt inside the Joule chatbot
  • Agents, which lets you define complex scenarios that the agent can execute by iteratively reasoning and interacting with their environment to achieve a goal, using a selected LLM and triggered by a user's prompt inside the Joule chatbot

The session will include an introduction to the SAP AI tools and some basic concepts needed for working with Joule Studio—such as Generative AI, AI Core, AI Launchpad, document grounding, embeddings, and what is an LLM? etc. 

Prerequisites

  • Bring your own laptop with power adapter and have a supported Web browser of either Google Chrome or Microsoft Edge. Other browsers are not supported.
  • It can be helpful to bring a tablet or second laptop, if you have, to have the instructions on a separate screen.

Location and Date 

This is an in-person event only (not virtual) and is planned for Friday, June 26, 2026, from 10:00 - 16:00 local time in Hyderabad, India (IST). The language of the event and the content will be English. 

Event Address: Tata Consultancy Services, Blocks SDB1 & SDB2, Sy. No. 255 (Part Ibrahimpatnam Mandal, Adibatla, Telangana 501510. Google maps: https://maps.app.goo.gl/ndt4YV8baNWaZu3M9

  • The event is kindly being hosted by Tata Consultancy Services – THANK YOU!
  • The instructors are SAP Developer Advocates @Rekha_DR  and @shrinivasan_neelamegam 

Please note that spaces are limited! We can only accommodate 25 attendees. If you want to attend, register by clicking the Yes button in the Will you be attending? section above to the right. About 2 weeks before the event, you will be asked to confirm your attendance and will receive further instructions.

entry.title: Re: Is SAP Datasphere mandatory for SAP Business Data Clo...
entry.content:
In both cases is necessary datasphere.

The use of Dataproducts or Intelligent apps or the case you want to externalize your products with BDC Connect.

You need Object Store for data products and Object Store resides in Datasphere.

Datasphere is the BDC core.

entry.title: How to allocate Capacity Units (CU) to SAC user licenses
entry.content:
We are currently experiencing a shortage of SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) user licenses.
After consulting with SAP in advance, we were informed that we need to allocate our contracted Capacity Units (CU) to SAC user licenses.

Could you please advise us on the specific procedure for allocating CU to SAC user licenses and the necessary steps to take?

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud enables user-based authoriza...
entry.content:
Hello @Joey3, 

That’s unfortunate (and to be honest doesn’t seem logic to me) that Net Value cannot be hidden via access level configuration.

So, to summarise the solution to this issue:

User-based authorization control for sales staff can be partially achieved by using Authorization Group for Product as restrictions. However, the Net Value cannot be hidden via access level configuration as it is not part of the price elements area controlled by access levels (3744032 - Authorization restrictions in the field Net Value - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - SAP for Me)

👉  If you create an improvement request please link it here in a comment and ideally tag me – I would vote for it to support this topic 😊

Best regards,

Kerisha

entry.title: Re: How you can manage Polish KSeF Requirement in SAP S/⁠4HANA Cloud Private Edition & SAP S/⁠4H
entry.content: Additional fiscal data tab is not feasible in FB60 and FB70 T-codes. do we need additional configuration?

entry.title: Re: Step-by-Step: Calling S/4HANA Private Cloud Standard OData APIs with OAuth 2.0 from Client Tool
entry.content:
Hi @shusei_sekiya ,

Thanks for the blog.

I have a clarification: does this also apply to OData V4 APIs? If yes, are there any differences in the setup process? Specifically, I’m stuck at the step where I cannot see my service name listed under S_SERVICE: SRV_NAME, and I’m also unable to set HT for the SRV_TYPE.

 

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: 🇮🇳 Joule Studio: Create Joule Skills and Agents Without Coding (Hyderabad, India)
entry.content:
This is the all-new SAP CodeJam for Joule Studio, a new tool in SAP Build that lets you create extensions to Joule so users can use SAP's digital assistant in their custom workflows.

The CodeJam is intended to introduce you to the Joule Studio tool so you can create your own:

  • Skills, which let you create predefined execution flows, triggered by a user's prompt inside the Joule chatbot
  • Agents, which lets you define complex scenarios that the agent can execute by iteratively reasoning and interacting with their environment to achieve a goal, using a selected LLM and triggered by a user's prompt inside the Joule chatbot

The session will include a short, simple introduction to the SAP AI tools and some basic concepts needed for working with Joule Studio—such as AI Core, document grounding, embeddings, and what is an LLM?. You will also have the opportunity to build agents directly using Python.

Prerequisites

  • Bring your own laptop with power adapter and have a supported Web browser of either Google Chrome or Microsoft Edge. Other browsers are not supported.
  • It can be helpful to bring a tablet or second laptop, if you have, to have the instructions on a separate screen.

Location and Date 

This is an in-person event only (not virtual) and is planned for Wednesday, June 24, 2026, from 09:30 - 16:00 local time in Hyderabad, India (IST). The language of the event and the content will be English.

Event Address:  Bosch Global Software Technologies Private Limited, Sattva Knowledge Park, Tower 1, 14th to 19th Floor Raidurg Village, Serilingampally Mandal Ranga Reddy District, Hyderabad, Telangana, 500081

Google Maps: https://maps.app.goo.gl/jdstx227U4YHzABb6

  • The event is kindly being hosted by Bosch Global Software Technologies Private Limited – THANK YOU!
  • The instructors are SAP Developer Advocates  @Rekha_DR  @ajay_soreng @shrinivasan_neelamegam 

Please note that spaces are limited! We can only accommodate 35 attendees. If you want to attend, register by clicking the Yes button in the Will you be attending? section above to the right. About 2 weeks before the event, you will be asked to confirm your attendance and will receive further instructions.

entry.title: Re: Workflow Trigger for Credit Memo (Document Type DG) i...
entry.content:
In SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud (version 2502.602), it is not currently feasible to trigger a workflow specifically for Credit Memo postings (document type DG) created via the Create Outgoing Invoices app. Standard workflow capabilities in the Public Cloud are limited to predefined scenarios, and custom workflow triggers for this app are not supported. As of now, there is no published roadmap item confirming future support for this functionality, to my knowledge.
The only path forward is either external workflow automation or raising a roadmap influence request with SAP.

entry.title: Getting wierd error while creating communication arrangement
entry.content:
Hello All, any lead would be helpful. Getting some error while creating communication arrangement.

Regards, Ravi

entry.title: Re: Negative Values in Actual Cost (Global Currency) in M...
entry.content: Good day @rohini_dutta21

entry.title: Re: HANA does not retain table partitioning when a column...
entry.content:
Hi 

Thanks for the update . 

Adding a column does NOT merge the Partitions at Hana Client. 

 

I have performed the below test : 

1) Created a test table -

create table I1 ( a int, b varchar(10), c varchar(10))

 

2) Partitioned the table :

Alter table I1

PARTITION BY HASH (a)

PARTITIONS 1,

RANGE (b)

(PARTITION VALUE = '2024000',

PARTITION VALUE = '2024001',

PARTITION VALUE = '2024002',

PARTITION VALUE = '2024003',

PARTITION VALUE = '2024004',

PARTITION OTHERS);

 

 

3) Inserted the test data :

insert into I1 values (100,'2024000', 'Good');

insert into I1 values (200,'2024000', 'Good');

insert into I1 values (300,'2024000', 'Good');

insert into I1 values (400,'2024000', 'Good');

insert into I1 values (1000,'2024001', 'Good');

 

4) Checked the Table and partitions :

SELECT * FROM I1;

select * from M_CS_PARTITIONS where TABLE_NAME='I1'

 

5) Added a new Column :

ALTER TABLE I1 ADD ("NEW_C" VARCHAR(10) );

UPDATE I1 SET NEW_C = 'NEW_VAL';

 

6)Checked the Table and partitions :

SELECT * FROM I1;

select * from M_CS_PARTITIONS where TABLE_NAME='I1'

 

The same table partitions appeared .

 

~Gaurav
